forgeApp.enterText2: In order to understand your needs better, we would like to know how you typically use Forge
forgeApp.enterText4: Prototyping - trying out concepts and techniques
forgeApp.enterText5: Developing projects - Forge as your main dev tool
forgeApp.matomoText1: Forge features an AI coding assistant called Forge Copilot with Model Context Protocol (MCP) integration, which enables enhanced AI capabilities by connecting to external servers and processing your workspace data. By using Forge Copilot with MCP, you consent to the transmission of workspace files, code context, and development data to configured MCP servers and AI providers. MCP processes smart contract code, compilation artifacts, and deployment information to provide intelligent assistance. External MCP servers (including third-party services) may retain data according to their own policies. You can disable MCP at any time through AI assistant settings. For full details on data collection, security, and your rights, please review the MCP Terms and Data Privacy policy when prompted. Your approval is required to use Forge Copilot with MCP features.
forgeApp.matomoText1Old: Forge features an AI coding assistant called Forge Copilot that needs your approval to use.
forgeApp.matomoText2: We also use Matomo, an open-source analytics platform that helps us improve your experience.
forgeApp.mpOp1Details: Our use of anonymous analytics data helps us improve your experience by identifying bugs and important UX improvements. This data can also be used in overall statistics on Forge use in the wider Web3 ecosystem
forgeApp.mpOp1Tooltip: This is a necessary selection for a better experience of Forge
forgeApp.mpOp2Details: Forge believes your privacy is of primary importance. Your help in making Forge the best possible open source public good toolset is also very important. Approval of performance analytics tracking greatly helps us refine and improve the toolset. Rest assured, we will never share this data with any third parties.
forgeApp.mpOp3Details: Forge Copilot helps by offering code suggestions and improvements
forgeApp.originWarningUnstable: This unstable Forge alpha branch has moved to https://alpha.forge.creditchain.org.
forgeApp.originWarningAlpha: Welcome to the Forge alpha instance. Please use it to try out latest features. But use preferably https://forge.creditchain.org for any production work.
forgeApp.originWarningMoved: The Forge has moved to https://forge.creditchain.org. This instance of Forge you are visiting WILL NOT BE UPDATED. Please make a backup of your contracts and start using https://forge.creditchain.org
forgeApp.aiWorkspaceGenerating: Your workspace is being generated. Please wait while I generate the workspace for you. It won't be long.
forgeApp.aiChatPrompt.createFile: Let's create a new file together in the current workspace
forgeApp.aiChatPrompt.exploreCapabilitie: Create a list of all features and summarized MCP functionalities in a concise manner. Propose a few related prompts I can use to enhance my workflow.
forgeApp.aiChatPrompt.startLearning: I would like to learn Web3 development. Can you create a learning path for me based on Learneth with resources and projects to work on?. Do not generate URLs
forgeApp.aiChatPrompt.loadSkills: List all the skills available and their functionalities. Then, propose a few prompts to use those skills effectively. Ask the user to specify which skill they want to load, and load those skills accordingly.
forgeApp.aiChatPrompt.etherscan: Give me a brief introduction to Etherscan and how it can be used with Forge Copilot. Give me some examples prompts and if applicable show me how I could write prompts which could leverage The Graph with other mcp tools (like The Graph, Alchemy, etc...). Do not write a wall of text, let me ask questions. Propose me a few directions moving forward.
forgeApp.aiChatPrompt.thegraph: Give me a brief introduction to The Graph and how it can be used with Forge Copilot. Give me some examples prompts and if applicable show me how I could write prompts which could leverage The Graph with other mcp tools (like Etherscan, Alchemy, etc...). Do not write a wall of text, let me ask questions. Propose me a few directions moving forward.
forgeApp.aiChatPrompt.alchemy: Give me a brief introduction to Alchemy and how I can use it with Forge Copilot. Give me some examples prompts and if applicable show me how I could write prompts which could leverage Alchemy with other mcp tools (like TheGraph, Etherscan, etc...). Do not write a wall of text, let me ask questions. Propose me a few directions moving forward.
forgeApp.aiChatPrompt.ethernews: Fetch https://ethereal.news and sum it up. Propose me to detail a specific area.
forgeApp.aiChatPrompt.createDapp: I want to create a DApp frontend for a deployed smart contract. First check deployed contracts with get_deployed_contracts and the current chain with get_current_environment. Then ask only the required setup options: if the current prompt or tool result says Location is fixed, do not ask Location; otherwise ask Location Workspace (default) or Inline in /frontend. Always ask Base mini-app No (default) or Yes, and Design defaults, style notes, or a Figma URL. Do not ask Theme, Primary Color, DApp Title, Layout, or any extra design questions. After asking, STOP and wait for my next reply. Do not call generate_dapp or write files in the same turn as the setup question. After I answer, use defaults for skipped fields and call generate_dapp with setupOptionsConfirmed=true and setupOptionsSummary before writing any DApp files.
forgeApp.ai.status.generatingNewWorkspac:
Generating new workspace with AI.
This might take a few minutes. Please be patient...
